column4: Banded Face Pulls: Improves posture and strengthen your upper back—tap to watch now.
column12: Acute Shoulder Impingement, Acute Rotator Cuff Tear, Elbow Pain (acute)
column17: Improves rear shoulder and upper back strength for better posture
column18:
1. Stand facing the anchor with feet hip-width apart.
2. Hold the rope or band with both hands and extend your arms at eye level.
3. Pull your hands toward your face while flaring your elbows and squeezing your shoulder blades.
4. Slowly extend your arms back to the start and repeat.
column19: Pull shoulder blades together keeping chest tall and elbows level

column4: Arm Scissors: Activate your shoulders and improve shoulder mobility—tap to start.
column17: Enhances shoulder mobility and coordination through repetitive horizontal adduction and abduction
column18:
1. Stand tall with feet hip-width apart and arms extended straight ahead at chest height.
2. Cross one arm over the other, then quickly reverse so the opposite arm crosses on top.
3. Continue alternating in a controlled rhythm while keeping your core engaged.
column19: Keep shoulders relaxed and avoid excessive trunk rotation

column4: Banded Hammer Curls: Build forearm and biceps strength—tap to start. 💪
column17: Strengthens biceps and forearm muscles
column18:
1. Stand on the center of the band with feet hip-width apart.
2. Hold band handles with palms facing each other.
3. Curl hands toward shoulders while keeping elbows close to the body.
4. Lower slowly to the starting position.
column19: Keep elbows tucked and avoid swinging the torso

column4: Banded Biceps Curl: Strengthen and tone your biceps—tap to begin. 💪
column17: Builds elbow flexor strength and contributes to functional pulling movements
column18:
1. Stand on the center of the resistance band with feet hip-width apart.
2. Hold band handles with palms facing forward and elbows close to sides.
3. Curl hands toward shoulders by bending elbows without swinging the torso.
4. Lower slowly to the starting position with control.
column19: Keep elbows tucked to sides and lower weight slowly with control

column4: Arm Raises with Breathing: Strengthen your shoulders and improve arm control—tap to begin.

1. Stand tall with feet hip-width apart and arms at sides.
2. Inhale deeply while raising both arms forward and overhead.

4. Move in a slow, controlled rhythm.
column19: Keep shoulders relaxed and avoid arching the lower back

column4: Banded Lateral Walk: Strengthen your glutes and improve hip stability—tap to start.

column17: Strengthens hip abductors and improves lateral stability
column18:
1. Place a resistance band around thighs or ankles and stand with knees slightly bent.

3. Continue stepping for desired reps, then return in opposite direction.

column4: Banded Shoulder Internal Rotation: Strengthen your rotator cuff and improve shoulder stability—tap to begin.

1. Attach band at elbow height and hold it with elbow bent 90° and tucked at your side.

3. Return slowly to the starting position with control.
column19: Keep elbow close to body and avoid trunk rotation

column4: Banded Lateral Raise: Build shoulder strength and improve arm lift control—tap to start. 💪

1. Stand on the center of the resistance band holding handles at your sides.

column4: Banded Pull-Apart: Strengthen your upper back and improve shoulder alignment—tap to start.

column17: Strengthens upper back and improves scapular control
column18:
1. Stand tall holding a resistance band at shoulder height with arms straight.

3. Return slowly to the starting position with control.

column4: Banded Shoulder External Rotation: Strengthen your rotator cuff and enhance shoulder stability—tap to begin.

column17: Builds rotator cuff strength and supports shoulder stability during arm movements

1. Attach the resistance band at elbow height and hold it with elbow bent 90° tucked at your side.

column4: Cat–Cow: Improve spinal mobility and reduce back stiffness—tap to start.

column17: Improves spinal mobility and posture awareness
column18:
1. Start on all fours with hands under shoulders and knees under hips.

3. Continue alternating the movement in a slow controlled rhythm.
column19: Keep a neutral spine and move with control

column4: Bird Dog: Improve core stability and balance—tap to begin.
column12: Acute Low Back Pain / Lumbar Injury, Wrist Pain (acute)
column17: Enhances core stability and coordination between limbs

2. Extend opposite arm and leg while keeping hips and torso stable.
3. Return to start and alternate sides with control.

column4: Chair Squat: Improve sit-to-stand strength and knee control—tap to begin.

column4: Child's Pose: Gently stretch your lower back and hips—tap to begin.

column4: Chin Tucks: Strengthen deep neck flexors and improve head posture—tap to begin.

4. Release slowly and repeat for the desired repetitions.

column4: Cobra Stretch: Extend your spine and open your chest—tap to begin.

4. Lower your torso slowly back to the floor with control.

column4: Crescent Pose (Yoga): Stretch hip flexors while building lower-body balance—tap to begin.

column4: Dumbbell Lateral Raise: Strengthen side delts for improved shoulder stability—tap to begin.

column4: Dumbbell Shrugs: Build upper trapezius strength and neck support—tap to begin.

column18:

3. Press your hands straight out in front of your chest, resisting rotation from the band.
4. Pull your hands back to your chest with control.
column19: Brace your core and press slowly to resist rotation

column4: Dumbbell Hammer Curl: Strengthen your biceps and forearms with a neutral grip—tap to begin. 💪
column17: Strengthens forearms and biceps while enhancing grip strength
column18:
1. Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand with palms facing your torso.
2. Keep elbows close to your sides and curl the dumbbells upward.
3. Pause at the top and squeeze the forearms and biceps.
4. Lower the dumbbells slowly back to the starting position.
column19: Keep wrists neutral elbows close to sides and lift with controlled tempo

column4: Dumbbell Press Jack: Combine pressing strength with dynamic footwork—tap to begin. 💪
column17: Combines shoulder endurance and cardiovascular challenge for improved upper body stamina
column18:
1. Stand tall with feet together and hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height.
2. Jump feet outward into a wide stance while pressing both dumbbells overhead until arms are extended.
3. Land softly with feet together while lowering the dumbbells back to shoulder height.
4. Repeat the movement continuously for repetitions.
column19: Maintain a tight core and press smoothly without arching the lower back

column4: Dumbbell Renegade Row: Strengthen your back while challenging core stability—tap to begin. 💪
column17: Develops upper back strength while challenging core stability
column18:
1. Start in a high plank with hands gripping a dumbbell under your shoulders and feet wider than hip-width.
2. Brace your core and row one dumbbell toward your ribcage, keeping hips square to the floor.
3. Lower that dumbbell with control and immediately row the opposite dumbbell in the same manner.
4. Maintain a steady plank position and keep your body stable throughout each repetition.
column19: Brace core, hinge at hips and row without rotating torso

column4: Dumbbell Tricep Kickback: Isolate and strengthen your triceps with controlled extension—tap to begin. 💪
column17: Isolates triceps to enhance arm extension strength and upper limb control
column18:
1. Hinge at hips and bend knees slightly while holding a dumbbell in each hand.
2. Keep upper arms close to torso and hinge forward until torso is nearly parallel to the floor.
3. Extend forearms backward until elbows straighten and squeeze the triceps.
4. Slowly return the dumbbells to the starting position with control.
column19: Hinge at hips keep upper arm parallel to torso extend with control

column4: Side-to-Side Lunge: Strengthen inner thighs and improve lateral hip mobility—tap to begin. 💪
column17: Improves lateral leg strength
column18: "1. Step sideways. 2. Bend lead knee. 3. Push back to center."
column19: Keep chest upright.

column4: Dumbbell Chest Fly: Strengthen your chest with controlled arm opening and closing—tap to begin. 💪
column17: Increases pectoral strength and chest range of motion
column18:
1. Lie on a bench or the floor holding a dumbbell in each hand above your chest with palms facing each other and a slight bend in the elbows.
2. Keep the elbows slightly bent and open your arms out to the sides in a wide arc until you feel a stretch in the chest.
3. Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining the elbow bend and stable shoulder position.
4. Bring the dumbbells back together above your chest by squeezing the chest and following the same arc.
column19: Maintain a slight bend in elbows and lower with control to chest level

column4: Dumbbell Thruster: Build full-body strength by combining a squat and overhead press—tap to begin. 💪
column17: Builds full body power and improves coordination between lower and upper body
column18:
1. Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height.
2. Descend into a squat by pushing hips back and bending knees until thighs are near parallel.
3. Drive upward through the heels, extend hips, and press the dumbbells overhead until arms are straight.
4. Lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height and immediately begin the next repetition.
column19: Keep chest up and drive through heels as you press overhead

column4: Dumbbell Lunge with Bicep Curl: Combine lower-body strength with bicep activation—tap to begin. 💪
column17: Improves unilateral leg strength and balance while adding targeted biceps strengthening
column18:
1. Stand tall with a dumbbell in each hand at your sides and take a controlled step forward.
2. Lower into a front lunge until the front thigh is parallel and simultaneously curl both dumbbells toward your shoulders.
3. Pause briefly with the front leg planted and the weights at shoulder height.
4. Push through the front heel to return to standing while lowering the dumbbells, then alternate legs.
column19: Keep torso upright and knee aligned over front foot while curling

column4: Dumbbell Wrist Curls: Strengthen your forearms and improve wrist control—tap to begin. 💪
column17: Improves forearm strength and grip endurance
column18:
1. Sit on a bench and rest your forearms on your thighs with palms facing up, holding dumbbells.
2. Allow the dumbbells to hang just past your knees so your wrists are extended.
3. Curl your wrists upward by contracting the forearms, keeping the forearms stationary.
4. Lower the dumbbells under control to the starting position and repeat.
column19: Keep wrists neutral and curl with slow controlled motions
